/cwe/weakness/540.Name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code
/cwe/weakness/540.Description: Source code on a web server or repository often contains sensitive information and should generally not be accessible to users.
/cwe/weakness/540.ExtendedDescription: There are situations where it is critical to remove source code from an area or server. For example, obtaining Perl source code on a system allows an attacker to understand the logic of the script and extract extremely useful information such as code bugs or logins and passwords.

Entries.IntroText: The following code uses an include file to store database credentials:

Entries.BodyText: If the server does not have an explicit handler set for .inc files it may send the contents of database.inc to an attacker without pre-processing, if the attacker requests the file directly. This will expose the database name and password.

Entries.IntroText: The following comment, embedded in a JSP, will be displayed in the resulting HTML output.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Recommendations include removing this script from the web server and moving it to a location not accessible from the Internet.
